memories.content: Name is Teja Kummarikuntla. Based in the US. Originally from India.

Software engineer by profession. Previously senior ML engineer at a Fortune 500 tech company for 4 years. Led a team of 5 engineers. Built a recommendation engine that increased user engagement by 35%.
Primary languages: TypeScript and Python. Deep expertise in machine learning, NLP, and LLM systems. Has built production ML pipelines serving millions of users.

Currently building HCP (Human Context Protocol) — a product that gives people a portable AI identity that works across every AI tool. Currently in MVP stage, preparing to pitch to investors and judges.

Prefers direct, concise answers — no fluff. Hates when AI is verbose or gives generic advice. Wants the AI to challenge his assumptions and push back when he's wrong. Values practical output over theoretical discussion.

When coding, prefers functional patterns and minimal comments. Likes explicit error handling. Naming convention: descriptive variable names. Architecture: event-driven, monolith-first, then extract services.

Systems thinker — always looks at the full picture before zooming in. First-principles thinker who questions assumptions. Wants to understand the 'why' before jumping into solutions. High energy, moves fast, iterates quickly.
Proficient with MCP (Model Context Protocol), Claude API, OpenAI API, vector databases, and agent frameworks. Has hands-on experience building MCP servers from scratch.

Writes in a direct, no-fluff style. Uses short punchy sentences. Avoids jargon unless talking to technical peers. Prefers concrete examples over abstract theory. When writing professional content (LinkedIn, emails), leads with the problem, then the insight, then the ask. Keeps paragraphs to 2-3 sentences max.

Short-term: Nail the HCP pitch, get into a top accelerator or close a pre-seed round. Medium-term: Make HCP the standard for user-owned AI identity. Long-term: Build a company that changes how humans and AI interact.
Deeply interested in protocol design, developer tools, the future of human-AI interaction, and identity/privacy in the AI era. Follows AI research closely.

Has published technical blog posts on Medium about ML systems and LLM applications. Gave a talk at a local AI meetup about building personalized AI agents. Active on Twitter/X sharing thoughts on AI products.
Strong product intuition — pivots ideas mid-conversation when he sees a better angle. Not afraid to throw away work if the direction is wrong. Values speed of learning over perfection.

Competitive landscape: Mem0 ($24M raised, 47K GitHub stars) is the biggest player in AI memory. HCP differentiates by being USER-owned identity (not developer-integrated memory). No one else does portable, user-facing AI identity.

summary: Teja Kummarikuntla is a founder and former senior ML engineer building HCP (Human Context Protocol) — a product that gives people a portable AI identity across every AI tool. He's a systems thinker who communicates directly, hates fluff, and moves fast. Deep technical expertise in ML/NLP, Python, TypeScript, and LLM systems. Previously led a team of 5 at a Fortune 500, built recommendation engines at scale. Currently preparing to pitch HCP to investors and judges. He wants AI to treat him as a specific person with specific patterns, not a generic user. His writing style is punchy, concrete, and leads with the problem. He values practical output, challenges to his thinking, and speed over perfection.
